publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-11-19 18:24:43 -0500.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Research files from John D'Emilio's book "Lost prophet: the life and times of Bayard Rustin".

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Research files from John D'Emilio's book "Lost prophet: the life and times of Bayard Rustin".

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Folder titles were recorded as they appeared on the original physical folders, which were divised by the creator of the collection. In cases where folders were labeled "BRINTER", processing staff recorded the title as "Bayard Rustin interview". Folders recorded as "Untitled" were originally left blank by the creator or contained material that was found loose in the box.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Material in this collection was used to create "Lost prophet, the life and times of Bayard Rustin", E185.97.R93 D46 2003.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John D'Emilio papers, #7879. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John D'Emilio (born 1948) is a professor emeritus of history and of women's and gender studies at the University of Illinois at Chicago. He earned his Ph.D. from Columbia University in 1982. He first taught at the University of North Carolina at Greensboro. He was a National Endowment for the Humanities fellow in 1997 and a Guggenheim fellow in 1998. In 1989, he joined the Advisory Committee for Cornell's new Human Sexuality Collection and helped shape its direction and success. He served as Director of the Policy Institute at the National Gay and Lesbian Task Force (now named the National LGBTQ Task Force) from 1995 to 1997. He wrote two important collections of essays:Making Trouble: "Essays on Gay History, Politics, and the University", and "The World Turned: Essays on Gay History, Politics, and Culture".
